finding: Non-numeric started_at_utc -> time.time()-str TypeError not caught by (JSONDecodeError
evidence: OSError); scan_runs builds RunInfo per-entry with no guard so one bad run crashes whole status/inspect
disposition: Coerce/validate numeric fields + per-run try/except in scan_runs

finding: runs.sort key started_at_utc or 0 raises TypeError str<int if value is a string
_format_timestamp datetime.fromtimestamp(epoch) guards only None; string/NaN/negative/out-of-range raises and crashes render
signals_received iterated as list-of-dicts (.get) and len(); non-dict/non-list entry raises AttributeError/TypeError uncaught
proposed_action: Guard iteration and len against wrong types

finding: os.kill(pid
evidence: 0) unguarded for pid<=0 (POSIX process-group semantics) and OverflowError for huge pid -> wrong RUNNING verdict driving close_out_crashed_run
disposition: Return False for pid<=0 and catch OverflowError

finding: expected_script in cmdline substring match false-positives on recycled PID or generic token (python/-c)
proposed_action: Stricter basename/word-boundary match; require non-generic token

finding: is_same_process returns True (alive) when process start-time unreadable -> reused PID treated as original
proposed_action: Do not default to alive; fall back to age/hostname heuristic

finding: sorted(records key name.lower()) outside try; None dist Name -> None.lower() propagates -> run ghosted (full-environment/top-level modes; default imported-only safe)
proposed_action: Move sorted inside try or null-guard name

finding: manual_subprocess_records uncapped unlike SubprocessSpy._records -> unbounded growth/OOM under tight loops
hardware.py subprocess.check_output has no timeout; hung nvidia-smi/system_profiler orphans thread+child leaves non-terminal pending hardware state in manifest
proposed_action: Add configurable timeout; terminal 'timeout' state on timeout

finding: macOS/Windows RSS pollers and macOS tree ps have no timeout -> orphaned watcher thread+child on hang
Three consecutive poll failures (any 0 RSS incl transient errors) permanently stop telemetry with no recovery -> under-reported peak on long runs
proposed_action: Count only raised exceptions toward threshold and/or raise threshold

finding: Hardcoded git timeout=1 -> slow/large repo silently recorded as 'Not a git repository'
proposed_action: Configurable larger timeout; distinguish timeout from no-repo

finding: tomllib.loads in load_local_config/load_user_config unguarded; malformed .pubrun.toml crashes CLI call sites (scan_runs) though Run.__init__ falls back
proposed_action: Try/except per file: warn and skip malformed file

finding: _restore_excepthook restores without identity check -> clobbers a later-installed hook (console.py does it right)
Passthrough original_stream.write only catches BrokenPipeError; other write errors (closed stdout) propagate out of tee write; non-default (capture_mode!=off)
status.py renders epochs in local time; diff.py renders UTC -> inconsistent/misleading timing for provenance
proposed_action: Standardize status on UTC and label zone

finding: unflatten_manifest scalar/dict prefix collision (e.g. package named numpy.core or env var with dot) -> TypeError; export json path broken (CLI catch prevents host crash)
List-diff x not in / .index uses == so True/1 alias -> wrong markers; env flatten assumes list-of-dicts and collapses duplicate names
proposed_action: Type-tagged value comparison; guard non-list inputs

finding: event_count = max(1
evidence: file_size//120) shown verbatim as Events:N with no estimate label; wrong for large/partial lines
disposition: Label as estimate or count cheaply

finding: pubrun.print sep.join(map(str
evidence: args)) before try; sep=None (accepted by builtin print) raises AttributeError; only reachable via explicit pubrun.print (not in builtins)

finding: combined interleave sorts on timestamp string; empty-timestamp lines sort to top -> mis-ordered chronology
proposed_action: Stable sort with original-order secondary key

finding: elapsed_seconds from wall-clock time.time() -> negative under backward clock step; display handles it but persisted value negative
proposed_action: Note limitation or clamp with flag

finding: _log_pubrun_record check-then-set _pubrun_logged non-atomic -> double-append under concurrent wait/poll
pubrun.subprocess.run records nothing when wrapped call raises (unlike SubprocessSpy) -> missing provenance for failed manual subprocess
compare_manifests(ignores: List[str]=[]) mutable default argument (latent)
_make_handler runs full finalization (write_artifacts JSON I/O + hashlib + 2s thread join + lock) inside SIGTERM/SIG_DFL signal handler -> not async-signal-safe deadlock/hang risk
proposed_action: Redesign to defer finalization off signal context (dedicated IPD + characterization tests)
